ANDREW CHURCH

GEORGE E MC INNIS
ARNOLD 8 MYERS
RICHARD B. ABRAIV\SON
PAUL D GULLION
WILLIAM H, STOFFERS, JR,
AN NE M. FRASSETTO
WONNE A, ASCHER
FAX (40A) 7sA-?O?e
November 5, 1990
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
Re: California Iceberg Lettuce Commission
Gentlemen:
The undersigned is the general counsel for the California Iceberg
Lettuce Commission and in that capacity I have had the opportunity
to review the Commission's Conflict of Interest Code in light
of the new statutory changes.
The Conflict of Interest Code provides for business positions;
however, does not provide for consultants. We have reviewed the
material which you sent to us regarding consultants and we believe
that the Conflict of Interest Code need not be amended at this
time; however, we would like confirmation from your office.
The California Iceberg Lettuce Commission was created by statute
in order to provide a vehicle to promote iceberg head lettuce
in the marketplace. The Commission has promotions with supermarkets,
tie-ins with food manufacturers, such as Kraft, and has some adver-
tising campaigns. In connection with these activities, 
the Commis-
sion hires a marketing firm that makes surveys of the consumption
of iceberg lettuce, 3s well as the types of promotional materials
that seem to appeal to supermarket store managers. They then
report back to the Commission the results of their findings. 
The
Commission also hires advertising firms to help with the design
and dissemination of the marketing materials. Llhile these people


Fair Political Practices Commis ion
November 5, 7990
Page 2
are independent contractors, they do not appear to be 'rconsultants"
within the meaning of the regulations, 
These firms do not make
any independent decisions but only report information to the Comrnis-
sion and the Commission in turn makes decisions based partly upon
that infornation.
It would be appreciated if you would review the Conflict of Interest
Code for the Calfiornia Iceberg Lettuce Commission and indicate
whether or not the Code needs to be amended in any respect.
Your cooperation is greatly appreciated and we look forward to
hearing from you .
Very t.
ours,
For ABRAMSON, CHiIRCH & STAVE

California Iceberg Lettuce Commiss ion

sections.question: The Conflict of Interest Code provides for business positions; however, does not provide for consultants. We have reviewed the material which you sent to us regarding consultants and we believe that the Conflict of Interest Code need not be amended at this time; however, we would like confirmation from your office.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the California Iceberg Lettuce Commission's Conflict of Interest Code need to be amended to address consultants, or are the marketing and advertising firms it engages (which do not make independent decisions) appropriately classified as something other than consultants?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The letter contains a question/request but no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections, as it is correspondence TO the FPPC requesting advice. No FPPC response content is present in this document.

embedding.summary: Incoming letter from counsel for the California Iceberg Lettuce Commission requesting FPPC confirmation regarding whether the Commission's Conflict of Interest Code needs to be amended to account for consultant positions, arguing that the marketing and advertising firms they hire are independent contractors but not "consultants" under regulations.
